custom_tools.standardization_utils package¶

Submodules¶

custom_tools.standardization_utils.gene_canonicalizer module¶

class custom_tools.standardization_utils.gene_canonicalizer.GeneCanonicalizer(species: str = '10090', use_mygene: bool = True)[source]¶

Bases: object

Canonicalizes gene identifiers (Ensembl, Entrez, aliases) to a preferred symbol using local files (GTF + NCBI/MGI gene_info). Species-agnostic; pass the correct files.

Parameters:

  • species (str) – Species identifier (e.g., “10090” for mouse, “9606” for human).

  • use_mygene (bool) – Whether to use MyGene.info for fallback symbol resolution (default: True).

canonical_symbol(s: str) → str[source]¶

Canonicalize a single gene identifier to an official symbol.

Parameters:

s (str) – Gene identifier (symbol, Ensembl ID, Entrez ID, alias).

Returns:

Canonicalized official symbol, or empty string if unmappable.

Return type:

str

canonicalize_series(s: Series, batch_size: int = 5000) → Series[source]¶

Canonicalize a pandas Series of gene identifiers to official symbols.

Parameters:

  • s (pd.Series) – Series of gene identifiers (symbols, Ensembl IDs, Entrez IDs, aliases).

  • batch_size (int) – Batch size for MyGene.info queries (default: 5000).

Returns:

Series of canonicalized official symbols.

Return type:

pd.Series

coverage_report()[source]¶

Generate a coverage report for the canonicalizer.

Returns:

Dictionary containing coverage statistics.

Return type:

dict

load_gtf(gtf_path: str)[source]¶

Load Ensembl GTF file to build Ensembl -> official symbol mapping.

Parameters:

gtf_path (str) – Path to the GTF file (can be gzipped).

load_ncbi_gene_info(gene_info_path: str, species_taxid='10090')[source]¶

Load NCBI gene_info file to build Entrez -> official symbol and alias -> official symbol mappings.

Parameters:

  • gene_info_path (str) – Path to the gene_info file (can be gzipped).

  • species_taxid (str) – NCBI taxonomy ID for the species (default: “10090” for mouse).

standardize_df(df: DataFrame, tf_col: str, tg_col: str) → DataFrame[source]¶

Canonicalize gene identifiers in a DataFrame.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Input DataFrame.

  • tf_col (str) – Column name for transcription factors.

  • tg_col (str) – Column name for target genes.

Returns:

DataFrame with canonicalized gene identifiers.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame
